URL: https://myfloridacfo.com/division/sfm/bfp/florida-fire-prevention-code

SUMMARY:
The Florida Fire Prevention Code is a complex set of fire code provisions. You can view and download the current and past codes here.

FULL CONTENT:
![CFO Seal in Gold](/sitefinity-images/default-source/assets/cfo_gold.png?sfvrsn=80d16a28_3)

# State Fire Marshal

## Florida Fire Prevention Code (FFPC)

The Florida Fire Prevention Code is adopted by the State Fire Marshal at three year intervals as required by Chapter 633.202, Florida Statutes. This complex set of fire code provisions are enforced by the local fire official within each county, municipality, and special fire district in the state. The county, municipality or special district in which you reside may also have local amendments applicable only to your community.

If you have a specific question regarding the Florida Fire Prevention Code or how it affects a specific building, occupancy or place of residence, please contact your local fire official. You may access an online "Read Only" version of the Florida Fire Prevention Code by clicking on the link below:

### 8th Edition of the Florida Fire Prevention Code

**[8th Edition (2023) FFPC Online & Purchasing](https://link.nfpa.org/free-access/publications/1and101/2023Florida)**(Effective date: December 31, 2023) \*Free NFPA registration required to access FFPC.

[Free Access to NFPA 855: Installation of Stationary Energy Storage Systems](https://link.nfpa.org/free-access/publications/855/2023)

[Florida Fire Prevention Code 8th Edition NFPA 101 (Florida Specific)](/docs-sf/state-fire-marshal-libraries/sfm-documents/bfp/florida-fire-prevention-code/florida-fire-prevention-code-8th-edition-nfpa-101-fl-sp.pdf?sfvrsn=a9bed124_2)

[Florida Fire Prevention Code 8th Edition NFPA 1 (Florida Specific)](/docs-sf/state-fire-marshal-libraries/sfm-documents/bfp/florida-fire-prevention-code/florida-fire-prevention-code-8th-edition-nfpa-1-fl-sp.pdf?sfvrsn=10321290_2)

### Florida Fire Prevention Code is now Accessible on NFPA Link

The 8th Edition of the Florida Fire Prevention Code is now fully available in the LiNK platform for subscribers. A couple of key notes about viewing the FFPC:

### Local Amendments to the Florida Fire Prevention Code

[Access any local amendment affecting your community](/division/sfm/bfp/local-amendments). Local amendments are enforceable only by the local jurisdiction and may not be appealed to the State Fire Marshal.

### **9th Edition Adoption Proceedings**

The Department of Financial Services, Division of State Fire Marshal, announces that it is seeking written comments and proposals for Florida specific amendments to the edition of NFPA 1 (2024 edition) and NFPA 101 (2024 edition).  The comment period begins February 24th,2025 and ends April 10th, 2025 at 5:00 pm.  Written comments should be addressed to: Elijah Flowers, Bureau of Fire Prevention, Department of Financial Services, Division of State Fire Marshal, 200 East Gaines Street, Tallahassee, Florida 32399-0340.

[PROPOSAL TO THE 9TH EDITION OF THE FLORIDA FIRE PREVENTION CODE](/docs-sf/state-fire-marshal-libraries/sfm-documents/bfp/florida-fire-prevention-code/9th-edition-ffpc-proposal-form-general.pdf?sfvrsn=4eb8a27d_2)

### Informal Interpretations of Florida Fire Prevention Code

Interested persons can seek and receive an informal non-binding opinion on requests involving questions related to the Florida Fire Prevention Code through the Florida Fire Marshal and Inspector Association.

To participate as a member of the Fire Code Interpretations Committee, print this form, complete the form, and return it to the State Fire Marshal. You may mail the form to Jenita Hicks-Zellars at 200 East Gaines Street, Tallahassee, FL 32399-0342 or you may
attach it in an e-mail to [Fire.Prevention@myfloridacfo.com](mailto:Fire.Prevention@myfloridacfo.com).

Our department manages the financial responsibilities for the State of Florida.

[200 East Gaines Street, Tallahassee, FL 32399](https://www.google.com/maps/place/J.+Edwin+Larson+Building,+200+E+Gaines+St,+Tallahassee,+FL+32301/@30.4351414,-84.2801365,17z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m5!3m4!1s0x88ecf5657b200879:0x7877e3b57a76058d!8m2!3d30.4351414!4d-84.2801365)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Fire safety regulations provide a framework to safeguard life and property against fire hazards, encompassing standards from OSHA, NFPA, and local codes.
Various standards, such as NFPA codes, International Fire Code (IFC), and local amendments, dictate minimum safety requirements essential for effective fire management.
Compliance with fire safety regulations is crucial to ensure safety, reduce liability, and enhance preparedness, ultimately protecting lives and property from fire incidents.
